name: Braised Country Style Ribs
snippet: I love Chicken, Fish and Turkey, but admittedly I grow tired of the usual suspects from time to time.  I told Joe that I wanted to mix it up a bit so we picked up a pack of Country Style Pork Ribs from the grocery store.  These are not typical ribs with bones that you eat with your fingers.  They are essentially shoulder (Boston) butts cut into strips and require a knife and fork.  Nevertheless, they are tasty grilled but even better in my opinion, cooked low and slow in the oven.  That is exactly how I decided to serve them up.  These ribs have just the right amount of fat, allowing them to come out juicy and tender after 3 to 4 hours of braising.  A classic comfort dish for sure, and just perfect for the colder months.  I am already looking forward to leftovers!
